channel.title: Pier — Release notes
channel.description: The Pier macOS app auto-update feed. Each item is one released version.

item.title: Version 1.1.2
item.description:
What's new in 1.1.2

Release automation: every future version ships with one command

  • One-command releases. Bumping Pier is now a single script — scripts/release.sh handles building, Apple notarization, DMG packaging, Sparkle signing, appcast + versions.json updates, deploy to pier.abn.company, and Vercel alias in one go. The sparkle:version gotcha that briefly broke the 1.1.0 → 1.1.1 upgrade is impossible to recreate by hand now.
  • Preflight safety. The release script refuses to run if the website working tree has uncommitted changes, the release-notes JSON is malformed, or the Ed25519 signature fails to verify against the freshly built DMG. Failing fast instead of shipping a broken update.

item.title: Version 1.1.1
item.description:
What's new in 1.1.1

  • Cleaner internal branding. The app's user-agent, startup log line, and server banner are now consistently "Pier" instead of the legacy "FTP Manager" string. Visible only in server logs, but long overdue.
  • First real auto-update test. If you're reading this, Sparkle just delivered this update to you — no manual download. That's the new normal from now on.

item.title: Version 1.1.0
item.description:
What's new in 1.1.0

  • SFTP over SSH. Alongside FTP and FTPS, Pier now speaks true SFTP over SSH. Pick "SFTP (SSH)" from the protocol dropdown when adding a connection — port 22 is filled in automatically.
  • Auto-update. Pier now checks for new versions in the background and offers to install them with one click. Powered by Sparkle. Weekly checks by default.
  • Check for Updates… menu item under the Pier app menu so you can pull updates manually whenever you like.
  • License paste now strips wrapped newlines — emails that word-wrap your key across two lines no longer cause activation errors.
  • Bug fixes: cleaner menu titles (Pier instead of FTP Manager), more robust error classification for authentication failures, improved signing of native SSH crypto binaries.
